data.title: Hawaiian Electric Industries Q4 Non-GAAP Earnings, Revenue Fall; Shares Drop After Hours
data.description:
Hawaiian Electric Industries reported Q4 non-GAAP core earnings late Friday of $0.20 per diluted share, down from $0.34 a year earlier.

Four analysts polled by FactSet expected $0.39.

Revenue in the three months ended Dec. 31 fell to $799.2 million from $853.4 million a year earlier.

Shares of the company fell 3.8% in after-hours trading.

data.title: Mexico's Inflation Seen Up in Early February — Market Talk
data.description: Mexico's inflation is expected to have picked up in the first half of February with a modest increase in prices comparing unfavorably with the decline in the year-earlier period. The consumer price index is seen rising 0.14% in the first two weeks of the month, according to a Wall Street Journal survey of analysts. That would push 12-month inflation up to 3.73% from 3.48% in the second half of January. Core CPI is expected to have risen 0.26% for an annual rate of 3.63%, little changed from 3.61% in the second half of January. Statistics institute Inegi is scheduled to report early February inflation on Monday. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: ATI Physical Therapy Inc expected to post a loss of $3.20 a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• ATI Physical Therapy Inc is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on February 24 (estimated) for the period ending December 31 2024

  • The Downers Grove Illinois-based company is expected to report a 2.0% increase in revenue to $185.905 million from $182.29 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 2 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for ATI Physical Therapy Inc is for a loss of $3.20 per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is no "strong buy" or "buy," 2 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for ATI Physical Therapy Inc is $3.00​, above​ its last closing price of $1.17. ​​​

data.title: Restaurant Brands International Files Mixed Shelf Registration
data.description:
Restaurant Brands International filed a registration statement with the US Securities and Exchange Commission for the potential sale of securities from time to time in one or more offerings by the company and selling shareholders.

The securities include common shares, debt securities and warrants, the company said Friday in the filing.

data.title: Informa TechTarget Announces Date of Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Financial Results Release, Conference Call, and Webcast
data.description:
Informa TechTarget Announces Date of Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Financial Results Release, Conference Call, and Webcast

Live Conference Call and Webcast Scheduled to Begin at 5:00 p.m. ET

NEWTON, Mass.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--February 21, 2025--

TechTarget, Inc. ("Informa TechTarget"), a leading growth accelerator for the B2B Technology sector, today announced details for the release of its fourth quarter and full year financial results. Following the combination of TechTarget with Informa Tech's digital businesses, which completed in December 2024, Informa TechTarget is currently classified as a non-accelerated filer and will announce its results after market close on Monday March 31, 2025. It will also file its Annual Report on Form 10-K on that date.

The Company's management team will host a live conference call and webcast at 5:00 p.m. Eastern Time on that day to discuss the Company's financial results. In conjunction with the announcement and the call, the Company will distribute an update on the business, current market conditions, operational, and financial results for the applicable period, and other matters, with the call being reserved for a summary of financial highlights by management and Q&A.

The financial results and a letter to shareholders will be accessible prior to the conference call and webcast on the investor information section of the Company's website at https://investor.informatechtarget.com.

Conference Call Dial-In Information:

  • United States (Toll Free): 1-833-470-1428
  • United States: 1-404-975-4839
  • United Kingdom (Toll Free): +44 808 189 6484
  • United Kingdom: +44 20 8068 2558
  • Global Dial-in Numbers
  • Access code: 034286
  • Please access the call at least 10 minutes prior to the time the conference is set to begin.
  • Please ask to be joined into the Informa TechTarget call.

Conference Call Webcast Information:

This webcast can be accessed via Informa TechTarget's website at https://investor.informatechtarget.com/

Conference Call Replay Information:

A replay of the conference call will be available via telephone beginning one (1) hour after the conference call through April 30, 2025 at 11:59 p.m. EDT. To hear the replay:

  • United States (Toll Free): 1-866-813-9403
  • United States: 1-929-458-6194
  • Access Code: 423695

About Informa TechTarget

TechTarget, Inc. , which also refers to itself as Informa TechTarget, informs, influences and connects the world's technology buyers and sellers, helping accelerate growth from R&D to ROI.

With a vast reach of over 220 highly targeted technology-specific websites and over 50 million permissioned first-party audience members, Informa TechTarget has a unique understanding of and insight into the technology market.

Underpinned by those audiences and their data, we offer expert-led, data-driven, and digitally enabled services that have the potential to deliver significant impact and measurable outcomes to our clients:

  • Trusted information that shapes the industry and informs investment
  • Intelligence and advice that guides and influences strategy
  • Advertising that grows reputation and establishes thought leadership
  • Custom content that engages and prompts action
  • Intent and demand generation that more precisely targets and converts

Informa TechTarget is headquartered in Boston, MA and has offices in 19 global locations. For more information, visit informatechtarget.com and follow us on LinkedIn.

(c) 2025 TechTarget, Inc. All rights reserved. All trademarks are the property of their respective owners.

data.title: Albany International Declares Dividend
data.description:
Albany International Declares Dividend

ROCHESTER, N.H.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--February 21, 2025--

The Board of Directors of Albany International Corp. today declared a quarterly dividend of $0.27 per share on the Company's Class A Common Stock.

The dividend is payable April 7, 2025, to shareholders of record on March 21, 2025.

About Albany International Corp.

Albany International is a leading developer and manufacturer of engineered components, using advanced materials processing and automation capabilities, with two core businesses.

  • Machine Clothing is the world's leading producer of custom-designed, consumable belts essential for the manufacture of paper, paperboard, tissue and towel, pulp, non-wovens and a variety of other industrial applications.
  • Albany Engineered Composites is a growing designer and manufacturer of advanced materials-based engineered components for demanding aerospace applications, supporting both commercial and military platforms.

Albany International is headquartered in Rochester, New Hampshire, operates 30 facilities in 13 countries, employs approximately 5,600 people worldwide, and is listed on the New York Stock Exchange (Symbol: AIN). Additional information about the Company and its products and services can be found at www.albint.com.

data.title: BDIC opens in HK and names Kohli MD
data.description:
(The Insurer) - Startup Blockchain Deposit Insurance Corporation (BDIC) has opened an operation in Hong Kong and appointed Paul Kohli as co-founder and managing director.

The new office, based in Hong Kong's Central District, is “an ideal location” for BDIC’s expansion in the city-state, the company said.

“Hong Kong has rapidly emerged as a global leader in the cryptocurrency sector,” it said.

“In 2024, the city witnessed an 85.6% year-on-year surge in cryptocurrency transaction value, the most considerable growth in East Asia.

“This impressive growth aligns perfectly with Blockchain Deposit Insurance Corporation's mission to provide comprehensive insurance solutions,” BDIC said.

The startup, led by Jeffrey Glusman, said the new Hong Kong office will serve as BDIC’s regional command center, supporting the firm’s planned expansion across Asia.

“Hong Kong represents a crucial milestone in BDIC's global expansion strategy,” said Glusman, BDIC’s global CEO.

“With its robust regulatory framework and sophisticated crypto ecosystem, Hong Kong provides the perfect foundation for our Pan-Asia operations,” Glusman added.

The new Hong Kong office will initially focus on providing deposit insurance coverage for licensed cryptocurrency exchanges.

BDIC then plans to expand its services to retail investors by the first quarter of next year.

“Hong Kong's cryptocurrency market has shown remarkable maturity and growth potential," said Kohli.

The new hire said BDIC's presence in Hong Kong puts the company in direct contact with key stakeholders in the digital currency space, allowing it to better serve their insurance needs.

Launch of the new office comes after BDIC launched its new cryptocurrency insurance platform, with the operation set to initially go live in select Asian markets during Q2 2025.

Initially, the startup’s insurance platform will launch in Hong Kong, Singapore, Japan, Taiwan and South Korea.

BDIC is also working on securing Lloyd’s coverholder status in a move that will allow it to underwrite cryptocurrency-related risks.

In late January, BDIC said that over the coming months it “will pursue Lloyd’s of London coverholder status”.

That designation, BDIC said, will allow it to develop robust insurance policies in collaboration with Lloyd’s syndicate members already servicing the industry.

data.title: Transport stocks take a hit on Wall Street on economic and tariff fears
data.description:
Key points:
  • Old Dominion loses 8.5%; Avis Budget falls 7.2%
  • Airline stocks also among the biggest decliners

Transportation stocks went sharply in reverse on Friday with the economic bellwether Dow Jones Transport average index falling more than 2.6% in its steepest daily loss since December 18 and its third consecutive decline, as investors reacted to weak economic readings and worried about tariffs.

While the transport index pared its losses by the close, most of its stocks fell for the day. Its biggest decliner was Old Dominion , finishing down 8.5%. Car rental company Avis Budget , the second biggest loser, fell 7.2%.

Airlines were among the biggest decliners, with Alaska Air Group falling 6.9%, United Airlines dropping 6.4% and Delta Air Lines ending down 5.9%. Also among the laggards were FedEx , which lost 5.3%, and Kirby Corp , down 3%.

Earlier, S&P Global data showed U.S. business activity nearly stalling in February and tumbling to a nearly 17-month low, with fears over import tariffs and deep federal government spending cuts, erasing soaring sentiment after the November U.S. election.

Also on Friday a University of Michigan survey showed U.S. consumer sentiment dropping more than expected in February to a 15-month low with inflation expectations jumping as households worried about plans for steep, broad-based tariffs.

And the data followed a downbeat forecast on Thursday from the world's biggest retailer, Walmart as it cited the need for caution in navigating an uncertain geopolitical landscape, sending its shares and the broader market down.

As for the drivers for the transport declines, Robert Pavlik, senior portfolio manager at Dakota Wealth, pointed to questions about the health of the economy and concerns about factors such as tariffs.

President Donald Trump in his first month in office slapped an additional 10% tariff on Chinese imports. While a 25% levy on imports from Mexico and Canada was suspended until March, this month he raised tariffs on steel and aluminum imports to 25%.

And on Tuesday Trump said he would impose auto tariffs "in the neighborhood of 25%" and similar duties on semiconductors and pharmaceutical imports.

The worry is that threats or implementation of tariffs will boost prices and hurt demand for goods and in turn the carriage of these goods by freight companies and home package deliverers.

"When one is to contemplate the impact of what will be shipped from other countries to the United States if, in fact, the new administration moves forward with tariffs, there might be a decrease of international deliveries and perhaps an increase of domestic delivery," said Art Hogan, chief market strategist at B Riley Wealth.

The broader market also reflected a risk-off mood on Friday with the three major Wall Street indexes losing more than 1%. The Nasdaq was leading losses with a 2.2% drop.

data.title: B Riley ends higher after release of 10Q
data.description:
** Shares of troubled investment bank B Riley Financial Inc turns higher to close up 10.4% at $3.40, pares gains after lifting of volatility halt, having risen as much as 33.1%

** RILY said in quarterly report on Friday it believes it has cash and cash equivalents, securities and other investments owned, funds available under our credit facilities, and cash expected to be generated from operating activities sufficient for capital and capital expenditure requirements for at least 12 months

** RILY down 24.9% YTD vs NASDAQ Composite Index's 1.1% increase

** RILY is looking for successors to the Nomura-led lending syndicate as part of a turnaround plan that may see the bank spin off its securities business, a Bloomberg report said this month

** Bank has been in a tailspin since last year when it suspended its dividend and revealed huge losses tied to Franchise Group bankruptcy, a retailer backed by RILY

** RILY and its co-CEO Bryant Riley have also received subpoenas from the U.S. SEC over the bank's dealings with Franchise's former CEO Brian Kahn

** Bank has sought to fortify its balance sheet and stem the stock rout through divestitures of non-core units

** RILY stock slumped 78.1% in 2024

data.title: H&E Equipment Services 4Q Rev $384.1M >HEES
H&E Equipment Services 4Q Net $32.8M >HEES
H&E Equipment Services 4Q EPS 90c >HEES
Global Commodities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Commodities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

1516 ET - Lean hog futures have been on a losing streak, following the drop-off seen in pork cutout prices in recent sessions. But there are signs that hogs could see a turnaround next week, in part due to cutout prices posting a turnaround this afternoon - and the outlook for pork in 2025 being generally strong. "The pork industry anticipates increased production, higher prices, and robust demand both domestically and internationally in 2025," says Ben Johnson of New Frontier Capital Markets. Lean hogs finished down 1% to 87.65 cents a pound, while live cattle futures rose 0.1% to $1.939 a pound. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1505 ET - Oil futures beat a Friday retreat for the second time in two weeks, giving up gains of previous sessions that were fueled by near-term supply concerns and expectations that OPEC+ may put off returning barrels to the market in April as planned. Traders have also been watching U.S. efforts for talks to end the Russia-Ukraine war. "An array of geopolitical, military and macroeconomic crosscurrents continues to limit upside follow-through in WTI to above the $73.50 level while restricting downside to the $70 area," Ritterbusch says in a note. WTI settles down 2.9% at $70.40 a barrel and Brent falls 2.7% to $74.43 a barrel, both down 0.4% on the week. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1458 ET - U.S. natural gas futures post a 14% weekly gain as an Arctic blast lifted heating demand across much of the country, while deliveries to LNG export terminals hit new highs above 16 billion cubic feet a day. Cheniere Energy's announcement of a first cargo from its Corpus Christi Stage 3 expansion was "big news for LNG," Gelber &Associates says in a note. While milder temperatures next week are expected to lower natural gas demand, and production is seen picking up after freeze-offs, prices are expected to find support in the growing storage deficit given this winter's hefty withdrawals. The Nymex front month settles up 2% at $4.234/mmBtu. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1429 ET - Front-month gold futures fell in today's session, although gold finished the week higher. The front-month contract settled down 0.1% to $2,937.60 per troy ounce for the day, but ended the week up 1.9%. This marks the eighth consecutive week where gold closed higher--the longest stretch of weekly wins since August 2020, when the streak lasted 9 weeks. The appetite for gold continues to come from central banks, says David Miller of Catalyst Funds in a note. "The biggest factor driving gold to $3,000 per ounce is massive coordinated buying by BRICS central banks," says Miller. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1409 ET - Wheat futures on the CBOT are up 0.5%, with traders looking to the Black Sea for indications on how prices should move. "The Russia quota is signaling a concern that traders have had about the quality of both the Russian and Ukrainian crop," says Phil Flynn of Price Futures Group. Flynn adds that other crops globally have struggled, tightening the overall supply and demand picture. "The global wheat market crop has been poor--not only in Europe, but… about the U.S. deep freeze and its impact on winter wheat." Corn is down 1.4% and soybeans slide 0.5%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1346 ET - Corn and soybean futures on the CBOT are down, as the weather outlook for the U.S. Corn Belt looks supportive for crops being planted this spring. Precipitation over the next two weeks is expected to be above average, says the Climate Prediction Center in its latest data. Analysts and traders believe the moisture will be beneficial for farmers when they plant crops. With traders reconsidering their outlook for the number of acres planted this spring, the weather forecast is a pressure point--corn is down 1.2% and soybeans are off 0.6%. Wheat is up 0.7% on Russia's export quota.(kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1332 ET - The number of rigs drilling for oil in the U.S. increased by seven this week to 488, the most since the third week of September, oil services company Baker Hughes reports. Oil rigs were down by 15 from a year ago. Rig directed at natural gas fell by 2 to 99 and were down by 21 from a year ago. Natural gas company EQT this week cited efficiency gains and strong well performance in raising its production guidance for this year, as well as bringing forward plans to drop one fracking crew at the end of 1Q. The company's 35% year-on-year increase in completed feet per day in the second half of last year "reveals how producers can improve gas supply without adding rigs," Eli Rubin of EBW Analytics said in a note.(anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1215 ET - Corn futures on the CBOT have had a strong week, but they are leading row crop futures down, amid speculation that U.S. farmers will plant more corn than initially anticipated. "Corn is clearly showing some signs of weakness setting in on several fronts," says Sterling Smith of StoneX. "First ideas about seeing bigger than expected corn acres are keeping the buyers to the sidelines… the solid recent run up is moving grain off the farm which is adding to the pressure." Most-active corn is down 1.1%, while soybeans fall 0.5%. Wheat is up 0.8%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1201 ET - Energy futures in general were the chief focus for commodities traders this week, says Ole Hansen of Saxo Bank in a note. Natural gas and oil futures trended higher — with cold weather globally helping propel gas while oil got a lift from concerns of supply curtailments. According to data compiled by Saxo Bank, energy futures gained over 4% this week, while metals and grains gained under 2% and softs lost roughly 1%. But overall, gains in the Bloomberg Commodity Total Return Index are outpacing the S&P 500 amid President Trump's tariff push, says Hansen. "The near future points to heightened uncertainty," he says. "So far, commodities have emerged as one of the winners amid increased demand for key raw materials, which could face supply challenges." (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1136 ET - Lean hog futures continue to slide, with the most-active contract down 0.7% in morning trade. If they stay on the downside, then it'll be the third straight day that the continuous contract has finished lower. Lower pork cutout prices have been weighing on futures, this after the CFTC reported a growth in managed money traders holding long positions in hog futures in its Commitment of Traders report last week. This week's move may mean that fund traders are pulling back, says StoneX in a note. "Have we flushed it enough?" the firm asks. "Long liquidation has been quite apparent, though fund positions — which we'll get an update on this afternoon — would still remain on the upper end of historicals." Most-active live cattle is up 0.2%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1108 ET - Base metal prices fall, with LME three-month copper down 0.2% at $9,539 a metric ton and LME three-month aluminum down 1.6% at $2,686 a ton. Prices remain elevated however, with copper up 0.85% and aluminum up 1.9% on week. President Trump's tariff policies prompted a squeeze in New York traded copper prices, which saw futures prices spike to a nine-month high last week. This widened the U.S. premium over LME prices to more than $1,000 a ton, Saxo Bank's Ole Hansen says in a note. Since then, prices have started to drift lower with the premium narrowing, though it remains significantly elevated, Hansen writes. Recent copper market actions have been mostly about U.S. commodities exchange Comex buying to preempt tariffs, more than actual end-user demand, Hansen adds. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

1025 ET - Following a 27% devaluation against the dollar in 2024, the Brazilian real is up 8% this year. A report by BNP Paribas says that local funds decreased their BRL short positions to a negative 6.4% on February 17 from a negative 9.3% on January 31. The funds' foreign asset long positioning fell to 2.2% from 6.3%, BNPP says. The bank adds the move coincided with dollar weakness "and the removal of excess premium" after a BRL selloff in December. Over the past year, concerns about increasing government spending have been behind the currency weakness. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

data.title: Extra Space Storage Inc. Announces 1st Quarter 2025 Dividend
data.description:
Extra Space Storage Inc. Announces 1st Quarter 2025 Dividend

PR Newswire

SALT LAKE CITY, Feb. 21, 2025

SALT LAKE CITY, Feb. 21, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — Extra Space Storage Inc. (the "Company") announced today that the Company's board of directors has declared a first quarter 2025 dividend of $1.62 per share on the common stock of the Company. The dividend is payable on March 31, 2025, to stockholders of record at the close of business on March 14, 2025.

About Extra Space Storage Inc.

Extra Space Storage Inc., headquartered in Salt Lake City, is a fully integrated, self-administered and self-managed real estate investment trust, and a member of the S&P 500. As of December 31, 2024, the Company owned and/or operated 4,011 self-storage properties, which comprise approximately 2.8 million units and approximately 308.4 million square feet of rentable storage space operating under the Extra Space brand. The Company offers customers a wide selection of conveniently located and secure storage units across the country, including boat storage, RV storage and business storage. It is the largest operator of self-storage properties in the United States.

For more information, please visit www.extraspace.com.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/extra-space-storage-inc-announces-1st-quarter-2025-dividend-302382602.html

SOURCE Extra Space Storage, Inc.

/CONTACT: Jared Conley, Extra Space Storage, 801-365-1759, info@extraspace.com

data.title: DuPont Names Kurt McMaken to Board
DuPont Announces Appointment of Kurt McMaken to Board of Directors
data.description:
DuPont Announces Appointment of Kurt McMaken to Board of Directors

PR Newswire

WILMINGTON, Del., Feb. 21, 2025

WILMINGTON, Del., Feb. 21, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — DuPont today announced the appointment of Kurt McMaken to its Board of Directors, effective immediately. Mr. McMaken will serve on the Audit committee and Nomination and Governance committee.

"We are pleased to welcome Kurt to DuPont's Board of Directors," said DuPont Executive Chairman Ed Breen. "Kurt's global business expertise in the manufacturing sector and extensive background in finance and strategic planning make him well suited to help guide DuPont's growth and value creation strategies. We look forward to his contributions to the Board."

Mr. McMaken is Chief Financial Officer of Brinks, a leading global provider of cash and valuables management, digital retail solutions, and ATM managed services. Prior to joining Brink's in 2022, he was senior vice president, Operations Finance and Finance Transformation at Eaton Corporation plc, following a 20-year career with Eaton in financial and management positions. Mr. McMaken began his career in the Audit & Business Advisory Services practice at PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP.

Mr. McMaken holds a Bachelor of Science degree from Georgetown University and an M.B.A. from the University of Chicago Booth School of Business.

About DuPont

DuPont is a global innovation leader with technology-based materials and solutions that help transform industries and everyday life. Our employees apply diverse science and expertise to help customers advance their best ideas and deliver essential innovations in key markets including electronics, transportation, construction, water, healthcare and worker safety. More information about the company, its businesses and solutions can be found at www.dupont.com. Investors can access information included on the Investor Relations section of the website at investors.dupont.com.

DuPont(TM), the DuPont Oval Logo, and all trademarks and service marks denoted with (TM), (SM) or (R) are owned by affiliates of DuPont de Nemours, Inc. unless otherwise noted.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/dupont-announces-appointment-of-kurt-mcmaken-to-board-of-directors-302382517.html

SOURCE DuPont

/CONTACT: Dan Turner, (302) 299-7628, daniel.a.turner@dupont.com

data.title: Applied Optoelectronics Announces Equity Grants To Employees Under Inducement Plan
data.description:
SUGAR LAND, Texas, Feb. 21, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. (NASDAQ: AAOI), a leading provider of fiber-optic access network products for the internet datacenter, cable broadband, telecom and fiber-to-the-home (FTTH) markets, today announced that the Compensation Committee of its Board of Directors granted inducement awards for 6,812 shares of common stock to 4 new employees, with a grant date of February 21, 2025, pursuant to the Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. 2023 Equity Inducement Plan (the “Inducement Plan”).

The Inducement Plan is used exclusively for the grant of equity awards to individuals who were not previously employees of Applied Optoelectronics, or following a bona fide period of non-employment, as an inducement material to such individuals’ entering into employment with Applied Optoelectronics, pursuant to Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4).

The inducement awards are comprised of restricted stock units which vest over a four-year period, subject to the employee’s continued service on the applicable vesting dates. The restricted stock units are subject to the terms and conditions of the Inducement Plan and restricted stock unit agreements covering the grants.

About Applied Optoelectronics

Applied Optoelectronics Inc. (AOI) is a leading developer and manufacturer of advanced optical products, including components, modules and equipment. AOI's products are the building blocks for broadband fiber access networks around the world, where they are used in the internet datacenter, CATV broadband, telecom and FTTH markets. AOI supplies optical networking lasers, components and equipment to tier-1 customers in all four of these markets. In addition to its corporate headquarters, wafer fab and advanced engineering and production facilities in Sugar Land, TX, AOI has engineering and manufacturing facilities in Taipei, Taiwan and Ningbo, China. For additional information, visit www.ao-inc.com.

Investor Relations Contacts:

The Blueshirt Group, Investor Relations

Lindsay Savarese

+1-212-331-8417

ir@ao-inc.com

Cassidy Fuller

+1-415-217-4968

ir@ao-inc.com

data.title: Alta Equipment Group Announces Date of Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Financial Results Release, Conference Call and Webcast
data.description:
LIVONIA, Mich., Feb. 21, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Alta Equipment Group Inc. (“Alta” or “the Company”), a leading provider of premium material handling, construction and environmental processing equipment and related services, today announced that it will report its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2024, after the U.S. markets close on Wednesday, March 5, 2025. In conjunction with this announcement, Alta management will host a conference call and webcast that afternoon at 5:00 p.m. Eastern Time to discuss and answer questions about the Company’s financial results. Prior to the conference call and webcast, Alta will issue a press release and supplementary presentation slides reporting these results on the Investors portion of the Company’s website, https://investors.altaequipment.com.

Conference Call Details:

What: Alta Equipment Group Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Earnings Call and Webcast

Date: Wednesday, March 5, 2024

Time: 5:00 p.m. Eastern Time

Live call: (833) 470-1428

International: Global Dial-In Number: (404) 975-4839

Live call access code: 626132

Audio Replay: (866) 813-9403

Replay access code: 909287

Webcast: https://events.q4inc.com/attendee/852719845

The audio replay will be archived through March 19, 2025.

About Alta Equipment Group Inc.

Alta owns and operates one of the largest integrated equipment dealership platforms in North America. Through our branch network, the Company sells, rents, and provides parts and service support for several categories of specialized equipment, including lift trucks and other material handling equipment, heavy and compact earthmoving equipment, crushing and screening equipment, environmental processing equipment, cranes and aerial work platforms, paving and asphalt equipment, other construction equipment and allied products. Alta has operated as an equipment dealership for 40 years and has developed a branch network that includes over 85 total locations across Michigan, Illinois, Indiana, Ohio, Pennsylvania, Massachusetts, Maine, Connecticut, New Hampshire, Vermont, Rhode Island, New York, Virginia, Nevada and Florida and the Canadian provinces of Ontario and Quebec. Alta offers its customers a one-stop-shop for their equipment needs through its broad, industry-leading product portfolio. More information can be found at www.altg.com.

data.title: Equities Get Hammered as Traders Go Risk-Off - US Market Wrap
data.description:
Stocks fell after weaker-than-expected economic statistics fuelled concerns about the prospects for corporate America, amid a spike in consumers’ long-term inflation expectations to the highest level since 1995.

Investors were uneasy about Friday’s data on consumer mood, housing, and services, despite the fact that the Federal Reserve is not in a hurry to decrease interest rates. The S&P 500 fell more than 1.5%, while bonds rose. A total of $2.7 trillion in options connected to equities and ETFs were set to expire. This typically accentuates price movements. COVID-19 vaccine producers rallied as traders published prior information about a new coronavirus research in China, adding to the volatility.

The S&P 500 dropped 1.7%. The Nasdaq 100 declined 2.1%. The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 1.7%, followed by a slump in UnitedHealth. The Dow Transportation Index declined 2.6%. The Russell 2000 fell 2.9%. A gauge of the Magnificent Seven megacaps fell 2.5%.

A late rebound in the Treasury market sent the 10-year note yield lower for the sixth week in a row, as traders seek shelter despite falling stock and oil prices. The yield on 10-year notes declined by eight basis points, to 4.43%. The dollar index increased 0.2%.

data.title: Guardian Provides Update re. Action Against Site Exploration Mining Company
data.description:
(TheNewswire)

Calgary, Alberta – TheNewswire - February 21, 2025 - Guardian Exploration Inc. (OTCQB: GXUSF) (Frankfurt: R6B) (“Guardian” or the“Corporation”) reports that Site Exploration Mining Company(“SEMC”) David Ransom and CameronPetricevic have filed a counterclaim against the Corporation inresponse to the Corporation’s claim against SEMC and Messrs Ransomand Petrocevic announced on June 25, 2024.

The counterclaim seeks damages in the amount of$3,300,000, special damages to be proven at trial, interest andcosts.

The Corporation believes the counterclaim to befrivolous and without merit and has filed a statement of defence inresponse to the counterclaim.

About Guardian ExplorationInc.

Guardian is a TSXV listed company (OTCQB:GXUSF) (Frankfurt: R6B) in the business of oil and gas as well asmineral exploration and development.  Guardian’s first prospect isthe Mount Cameron Property located in the Yukon’s Mayo MiningDistrict.  Guardian also holds mineral claims located on southernDall Island, Southeast Alaska, USA, known as the Kaigani claims, whichit acquired in February 2022.

FOR FURTHER INFORMATION, PLEASECONTACT:

Graydon Kowal

President and CEO

(403) 730-6333

Neither the TSXVenture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term isdefined in the policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) acceptsresponsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

Such factors include but are not limited to operationalmatters, historical trends, current conditions and expected futuredevelopments, as well as other considerations that are believed to beappropriate in the circumstances.  There can be no assurance thatsuch statements will prove to be accurate, as actual results andfuture events could differ materially from those anticipated in suchstatements. data.title: Restaurant Brands International Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Restaurant Brands International Inc. (RBI), one of the world's largest quick service restaurant companies, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the competitive quick service restaurant industry. RBI operates over 32,000 restaurants across more than 120 countries and territories, owning and franchising four major brands: Tim Hortons, Burger King, Popeyes, and Firehouse Subs.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: $8,406 million, driven by acquisitions and system-wide sales growth across franchisor segments, partially offset by unfavorable FX Impact.

Income from Operations: $2,419 million, increased due to higher segment income and favorable changes in other operating expenses.

Net Income: $1,445 million, decreased primarily due to income tax expense compared to a benefit in the prior year and increased loss on early extinguishment of debt.

Diluted EPS: Not explicitly stated in the provided sections.

Business Highlights

Company Overview: Restaurant Brands International, Inc. (RBI) is one of the world's largest quick service restaurant companies, operating over 32,000 restaurants across more than 120 countries and territories. The company owns and franchises four major brands: Tim Hortons, Burger King, Popeyes, and Firehouse Subs.

Franchise Model: Approximately 95% of RBI's system-wide restaurants are franchised. The company believes that operating a limited number of company-owned restaurants helps in testing new products and operational standards before system-wide implementation.

Brand Performance: Tim Hortons is the largest coffee and baked goods chain in Canada, Burger King is the second-largest quick service hamburger chain globally, Popeyes is the second-largest quick service chicken concept, and Firehouse Subs is a leading player in the sandwich category in North America.

Geographical Performance: As of December 31, 2024, Tim Hortons operates in 22 countries, Burger King in 125, Popeyes in 47, and Firehouse Subs in 7. The international segment, which includes operations outside the U.S. and Canada, showed a system-wide sales growth of 10.0% in 2024.

Sales Units: The total system restaurant count at the end of 2024 was 32,125, with Tim Hortons having 6,043 restaurants, Burger King 19,732, Popeyes 4,979, and Firehouse Subs 1,371.

Operational Strategy: RBI's strategy focuses on three core pillars: Quality, Service, and Convenience. The company aims to enhance guest experience through training, technology, and restaurant reimaging, while also expanding its global footprint.

Future Outlook: RBI plans to increase the pace of remodels for Burger King restaurants acquired in the Carrols Acquisition and accelerate net restaurant growth at Popeyes China and Firehouse Subs Brazil. The company is also working to find new partners for these operations.

Sustainability Initiatives: RBI is committed to sustainability through its 'Restaurant Brands for Good' plan, focusing on food quality, environmental footprint reduction, and community support.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: In 2024, the company completed the acquisition of Carrols Restaurant Group Inc. and Popeyes China, expanding its operational footprint. The company also acquired the remaining equity interests in Burger King China, with plans to find a new controlling shareholder. These acquisitions are part of a broader strategy to optimize costs and leverage global scale while maintaining brand independence.

Capital Management: The company increased its Term Loan B by $750 million to fund the Carrols acquisition and refinanced a portion of this loan with the issuance of $1,200 million of 6.125% First Lien Senior Notes due 2029. Additionally, $500 million of 5.625% First Lien Senior Notes due 2029 were issued to redeem outstanding notes. The company maintained a share repurchase authorization of up to $1,000 million, with $500 million remaining under this authorization as of December 31, 2024. Dividends were increased to $0.62 per common share for the first quarter of 2025.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue its strategy of refranchising the majority of Carrols Burger King restaurants and finding new partners for Popeyes China and Firehouse Subs Brazil. It expects capital expenditures to total $400 million to $450 million in 2025, focusing on property and equipment additions. The company also anticipates increased cash taxes due to new Canadian tax legislation and plans to manage its debt obligations through strategic refinancing and interest rate swaps.

Challenges and Risks

Intense Competition: The restaurant industry is intensely competitive, with few barriers to entry. Competitors include independent operators and large chains, which may have greater financial resources and economies of scale, potentially impacting the company's market position.

Brand Value and Consumer Perception: The success of the company's brands depends on maintaining consumer trust and perception. Adverse publicity, health campaigns, or changes in consumer preferences could negatively impact brand value and financial results.

Economic Conditions: Economic downturns, inflation, and rising interest rates can reduce consumer discretionary spending, affecting restaurant sales and profitability.

Unforeseen Events: Natural disasters, pandemics, and geopolitical conflicts can disrupt operations, supply chains, and consumer behavior, leading to financial losses.

Global Operations Risks: Operating in diverse international markets exposes the company to regulatory, economic, and geopolitical risks, including currency fluctuations and compliance with foreign laws.

Supply Chain Vulnerabilities: Increases in food and commodity costs, or supply chain disruptions, could harm profitability. The company relies on a few distributors and suppliers, increasing the risk of supply interruptions.

Franchised Business Model Risks: The company's reliance on franchisees limits control over operations and exposes it to risks related to franchisee performance and financial health.

Labor Challenges: The company and its franchisees face challenges in attracting and retaining qualified employees, which could impact operations and profitability.

Regulatory and Compliance Risks: Changes in regulations, including labor laws and environmental policies, could increase operational costs and impact business strategies.

Information Technology and Cybersecurity: The company is vulnerable to IT system failures and cybersecurity threats, which could disrupt operations and lead to financial and reputational damage.

Indebtedness: High levels of debt could limit financial flexibility and increase vulnerability to economic changes.

Taxation Risks: Changes in tax laws and disputes with tax authorities could impact the company's financial results.

Management Strategies: Management acknowledges the challenges posed by intense competition and economic conditions. The company is focusing on enhancing digital engagement, improving service quality, and expanding its global footprint to mitigate these risks. Strategic acquisitions, such as the Carrols and PLK China acquisitions, are part of the company's growth strategy, but they also introduce additional risks related to integration and operational management.

Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in foreign currency exchange rates and interest rates, which could adversely affect financial results. The company employs risk management strategies, such as geographic diversification and derivative financial instruments, to mitigate these risks, but these strategies may not be fully effective.

SEC Filing: Restaurant Brands International Inc. [ QSR ] - 10-K - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: KFORCE INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Kforce Inc., a leading solutions firm specializing in technology, finance, and accounting staffing services, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in a competitive and evolving market.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $1,405.3 million, a decrease of 8.3% from $1,531.8 million in 2023, primarily due to macroeconomic uncertainty affecting client investments.
  • Gross Profit: $385.4 million, down from $427.1 million in 2023, with a gross profit margin of 27.4%, a decrease of 50 basis points from the previous year.
  • Income from Operations: $69.7 million, a decline from $87.1 million in 2023, reflecting tighter control on spending amidst revenue declines.
  • Net Income: $50.4 million, a decrease of 17.5% from $61.1 million in 2023, impacted by lower revenue and gross profit levels.
  • Diluted EPS: $2.68, down from $3.13 in 2023, reflecting the overall decline in net income.

Business Highlights

  • Company Overview: Kforce Inc. focuses on providing technology talent solutions and has streamlined its business to align with this goal.
  • Revenue Segments: The Technology segment represents 92% of Kforce's overall revenues, with the remaining revenue generated by the Finance and Accounting (FA) segment.
  • Consultant Placement: Approximately 18,000 consultants are placed annually with Fortune 500 and other leading companies, highlighting Kforce's significant role in the staffing industry.
  • Geographical Expansion: In 2024, Kforce established a development center in Pune, India, to support project engagements with U.S.-based clients, enhancing its offshore capabilities.
  • Technology Business Performance: Despite a 6.6% revenue decline in 2024, the Technology business remains a critical component of Kforce's operations, providing talent solutions in areas such as systems architecture, data management, cloud engineering, AI, and network security.
  • FA Business Strategy: The FA business has been repositioned to focus on highly skilled assignments, aligning more closely with the Technology segment to reduce susceptibility to technological change and automation.
  • Consultant Employment: The majority of Kforce's consultants are directly employed by the company, which includes domestic and foreign workers, supporting effective management of payroll taxes, benefits, and other labor costs.
  • Industry Position: Kforce is among the largest publicly-traded specialty staffing firms in the U.S., ranking as the sixth largest in technology temporary staffing and the tenth largest in finance and accounting temporary staffing.
  • Market Opportunity: Kforce is focused on expanding its market share in the U.S. technology temporary staffing industry and investing in higher-level technology services and solutions.
  • Strategic Priorities: Kforce's strategic priorities include back-office transformation, integrated strategy efforts, and evolving nearshore and offshore delivery strategies to enhance service offerings and operational efficiency.
  • Future Outlook: Kforce expects 2025 to be the final year of significant investments in strategic priorities, with meaningful returns anticipated in 2026 and beyond. The company is optimistic about leveraging its India development center to enhance service offerings.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: Kforce is prioritizing investments in strategic initiatives, including the implementation of Workday as part of its back-office transformation program, integrated strategy efforts, and the evolution of its nearshore and offshore delivery capabilities. These initiatives aim to enhance technology capabilities to better support clients, consultants, and candidates.
  • Capital Management: Kforce returned $64.7 million to shareholders through open market repurchases totaling $36.5 million and quarterly dividends totaling $28.2 million in 2024. The company reduced its outstanding debt under the Amended and Restated Credit Facility from $41.6 million to $32.7 million. Additionally, Kforce's Board declared and paid dividends of $1.52 per share in 2024, with an increase to $1.56 per share approved for 2025. The company also repurchased 609,000 shares for $36.5 million, with $63.5 million remaining available for further repurchases under the Board-authorized program.
  • Future Outlook: Kforce plans to continue exercising tight discretionary spend control while balancing productivity levels. The company expects to maintain sufficient liquidity for potential acquisitions or other strategic investments, supported by existing cash, operating cash flows, and available borrowings under its credit facility. Kforce anticipates stable Technology Flex gross profit margins and a decline in FA Flex gross profit margins in the first quarter of 2025. The company is also focused on capturing increased market share when the demand environment improves.

Challenges and Risks

  • Competitive and Fragmented Industry: Kforce operates in a highly competitive and fragmented staffing industry, which poses significant challenges. The company's focus on technology talent solutions increases its exposure to fluctuations in the technology sector.
  • Foreign Operations Risks: The establishment of a development center in Pune, India, introduces additional risks related to managing foreign operations and compliance with foreign laws.
  • Economic Sensitivity: The U.S. professional staffing industry is sensitive to economic fluctuations, and any downturn could reduce demand for Kforce's services.
  • Client Concentration: The concentration of revenues among large clients increases the risk of significant revenue loss if a major client reduces or terminates its business with Kforce.
  • Market Competition: The staffing market's low barriers to entry mean increased competition, potentially leading to pricing pressures.
  • Operational Risks: Kforce's strategic initiatives, such as the implementation of Workday and the expansion of nearshore and offshore capabilities, may divert management's attention and resources, posing operational risks.
  • Cybersecurity Threats: The company is exposed to cybersecurity threats, which could disrupt operations and damage its reputation.
  • Regulatory Risks: Potential changes in immigration laws affecting the availability of skilled foreign workers and the reclassification of independent contractors could increase costs.
  • Macroeconomic Uncertainty: The ongoing macroeconomic uncertainty has led to decreased client investment in digital transformation, impacting Kforce's revenue. The political landscape and geopolitical risks, such as Middle East tensions and global supply chain disruptions, add to the uncertainty.
  • Market Risks: Kforce is exposed to market risks, including interest rate fluctuations that could affect borrowing costs under its credit facility. The use of third-party software and AI introduces operational and compliance risks, as these technologies may not fully align with Kforce's business needs or regulatory requirements.

SEC Filing: KFORCE INC [ KFRC ] - 10-K - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: AGNC Investment Corp. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
AGNC Investment Corp., a leading provider of private capital to the U.S. housing market, has released its 2024 10-K report, showcasing significant improvements in financial performance, strategic initiatives, and operational highlights. The company, which primarily invests in Agency residential mortgage-backed securities (RMBS) on a leveraged basis, operates as a real estate investment trust (REIT) and focuses on generating favorable long-term stockholder returns.

Financial Highlights

  • Interest Income: $2,949 million, reflecting an increase from $2,041 million in 2023, driven by higher yields on investment securities.
  • Net Income: $863 million, a significant increase from $155 million in 2023, indicating improved profitability.
  • Net Income (Loss) Available (Attributable) to Common Stockholders: $731 million, compared to $32 million in 2023, highlighting a substantial improvement in earnings for common shareholders.
  • Net Income (Loss) Per Common Share - Diluted: $0.93, up from $0.05 in 2023, showing enhanced earnings per share.
  • Comprehensive Income (Loss) Available (Attributable) to Common Stockholders: $657 million, compared to $187 million in 2023, reflecting overall improved financial performance.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: AGNC Investment Corp. is a leading provider of private capital to the U.S. housing market, primarily investing in Agency residential mortgage-backed securities (RMBS) on a leveraged basis. The company operates as a real estate investment trust (REIT) and is internally managed, focusing on generating favorable long-term stockholder returns with a substantial yield component.
  • Investment Strategy: AGNC employs an active management strategy that is dynamic and responsive to evolving market conditions. The company focuses on maintaining a portfolio predominantly consisting of Agency RMBS, capitalizing on discrepancies in the relative valuations in the Agency and non-Agency securities market, and managing various risks including financing, interest rate, prepayment, extension, and credit risks.
  • Targeted Investments: The company primarily invests in Agency RMBS, which are considered a cornerstone of the U.S. financial system. AGNC also invests in non-Agency securities such as Credit Risk Transfer (CRT) securities, non-Agency RMBS, and commercial mortgage-backed securities (CMBS).
  • Financing Strategy: AGNC funds its investments primarily through collateralized borrowings structured as repurchase agreements. The company also utilizes TBA dollar roll transactions to finance the acquisition of Agency RMBS, which provides a form of off-balance sheet financing.
  • Risk Management: As a levered investor in fixed income securities, AGNC employs a variety of investment and risk management strategies to reduce exposure to market risks. The company continuously monitors and adjusts its hedge portfolio, net duration, and leverage to optimize returns over the long term.
  • Regulatory Compliance: AGNC operates to qualify as a REIT under the Internal Revenue Code and remains exempt from regulation under the Investment Company Act. The company must meet specific asset and income tests to maintain its REIT status.
  • Human Capital Management: AGNC emphasizes the strength, wellness, and dedication of its workforce, consisting of 53 full-time employees as of December 31, 2024. The company offers a competitive benefits package and maintains a low voluntary employee turnover rate.
  • Workplace Culture and Ethics: AGNC promotes a corporate culture of open communication, fair treatment, and high ethical standards. The company conducts regular compliance training and maintains policies against discrimination and harassment.
  • Employee Development: AGNC supports professional development through various programs, including professional certification reimbursement, one-on-one coaching, and a formal mentoring program.
  • Inclusion and Diversity: AGNC strives for an inclusive work environment, with 40% of its employees being women and 32% ethnically diverse as of December 31, 2024.
  • Compensation and Benefits: The company offers a pay-for-performance compensation philosophy, including base salary, annual cash bonuses, and long-term equity incentives. AGNC also provides comprehensive medical coverage and a 401(k) Savings Plan with company matching contributions.
  • Market Conditions and Outlook: In 2024, AGNC benefited from a favorable market environment as the Federal Reserve lowered short-term rates. The company anticipates a favorable outlook for Agency RMBS in 2025, with expectations of wide spreads relative to benchmark rates and potential increases in bank demand.
  • Operational Performance: AGNC's investment portfolio increased to $73.3 billion as of December 31, 2024, with a significant portion consisting of Agency RMBS. The company maintained an average leverage ratio of 7.2x tangible stockholders' equity in 2024.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: AGNC Investment Corp. has focused on enhancing its liquidity and capital resources by leveraging its investment portfolio, primarily through collateralized borrowings structured as repurchase agreements and TBA dollar roll transactions. The company has also raised $2.0 billion of common stock through its at-the-market offering program, which was executed at a premium to tangible net book value, thereby generating meaningful book value accretion for common stockholders.
  • Capital Management: AGNC's capital management activities include maintaining a leverage ratio between six and twelve times the amount of tangible stockholders' equity. As of December 31, 2024, the tangible net book value 'at risk' leverage ratio was 7.2x. The company has also focused on managing its repurchase agreement funding position through diversification of counterparties, maintaining a suitable maturity profile, and utilizing interest rate hedging strategies. Additionally, AGNC declared dividends totaling $1.44 per common share for the year 2024.
  • Future Outlook: Looking forward, AGNC anticipates a favorable outlook for Agency mortgage-backed securities in 2025, expecting Agency RMBS spreads relative to benchmark rates to remain wide, offering attractive return opportunities. The company plans to continue its active management strategy, which is dynamic and responsive to evolving market conditions, and aims to maintain adequate levels of liquidity and capital resources to support its operations and strategic objectives.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market and Operational Risks: AGNC operates primarily in the Agency RMBS market, which is subject to various risks including interest rate fluctuations, prepayment risks, and regulatory changes. The company's strategy involves leveraging investments, which inherently increases exposure to market volatility and financing risks. The company's reliance on short-term borrowings through repurchase agreements and TBA dollar roll transactions introduces additional liquidity and funding risks.
  • Investment and Portfolio Management Risks: AGNC faces significant risks related to its investment and portfolio management activities, including spread risk, interest rate and spread volatility, and the impact of the Federal Reserve's actions in the Agency mortgage market. The company's active portfolio management strategy may expose it to greater losses compared to passive strategies. Additionally, changes in prepayment rates and the fair value of assets can adversely affect financial conditions.
  • Financing and Hedging Risks: The company also faces risks related to its financing and hedging activities, such as the potential inability to procure or renew funding on favorable terms, increased borrowing costs, and margin calls that could force asset sales under adverse conditions.
  • Regulatory Risks: Regulatory risks include potential changes to the Federal conservatorship of Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac, which could impact the value and liquidity of Agency RMBS. The company must also comply with REIT requirements, which could limit its ability to hedge effectively and may require it to liquidate or forgo attractive investment opportunities.
  • Management's Discussion and Analysis: Management highlights the challenges posed by market volatility and the need to adapt investment strategies accordingly. The company actively monitors market conditions and adjusts its portfolio and hedging strategies to mitigate risks. However, there is no assurance that these measures will be sufficient to offset the negative effects of volatility on the business. The company also emphasizes the importance of maintaining compliance with REIT and Investment Company Act requirements to avoid adverse tax consequences and regulatory restrictions.
  • Market Risk Disclosures: AGNC is exposed to market risks, including interest rate risk, prepayment risk, and credit risk. The company's hedging strategies are designed to mitigate some of these risks, but they may not fully protect against all adverse changes. The effectiveness of hedging strategies can be impacted by factors such as the cost of hedges, correlation between hedge benchmarks and interest rate risks, and regulatory changes affecting derivative transactions. The company also faces risks related to the valuation of its investments, which may not reflect their realizable value in the event of forced liquidation.

SEC Filing: AGNC Investment Corp. [ AGNC ] - 10-K - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: ABBOTT LABORATORIES SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Abbott Laboratories, a global healthcare company known for its diversified portfolio in diagnostics, medical devices, nutritional products, and established pharmaceuticals, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report highlights the company's robust financial performance, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in a highly regulated industry.

Financial Highlights

Abbott Laboratories reported strong financial results for 2024, showcasing significant growth across key metrics:

  • Net Sales: $41,950 million, reflecting a 4.6% increase from 2023, driven by higher sales in the Medical Devices, Established Pharmaceutical Products, and Nutritional Products segments, partially offset by a decrease in demand for COVID-19 tests.
  • Gross Profit: $20,366 million, representing 50.9% of net sales, up from 50.3% in 2023 due to favorable impacts of margin improvement initiatives.
  • Operating Earnings: $6,825 million, an increase from $6,478 million in 2023, driven by higher sales volumes and margin improvement initiatives.
  • Net Earnings: $13,402 million, significantly up from $5,723 million in 2023, primarily due to a $7.5 billion non-cash valuation allowance adjustment related to deferred tax assets.
  • Basic Earnings Per Common Share: $7.67, compared to $3.28 in 2023, reflecting the substantial increase in net earnings.
  • Diluted Earnings Per Common Share: $7.64, up from $3.26 in 2023, consistent with the increase in net earnings.

Business Highlights

Abbott's diverse business segments and geographical reach contributed to its strong operational performance in 2024:

  • Revenue Segments: Abbott operates through four main segments: Established Pharmaceutical Products, Diagnostic Products, Nutritional Products, and Medical Devices. Each segment contributes to the company's diverse healthcare product offerings.
  • Geographical Performance: Sales in emerging markets, which represent approximately 37% of total company sales, increased by 8.2% in 2024, driven by higher revenue in Latin America, Southeast Asia, and the Middle East.
  • New Product Launches: In 2024, Abbott launched several new products, including the Lingo® and Libre Rio™ CGM systems, the Esprit™ BTK system, and the TriClip® for tricuspid regurgitation. These innovations reflect Abbott's commitment to expanding its product portfolio.
  • Sales Units: Abbott's continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) systems saw significant growth, with sales totaling $6.4 billion in 2024, reflecting a 21.8% increase over the previous year.
  • Future Outlook: Abbott plans to continue investing in product development across its segments, focusing on areas such as diagnostics, medical devices, and nutritional products to drive sustainable growth.
  • Operational Efficiency: Abbott implemented margin improvement initiatives in 2024, which positively impacted its operating margin profile, despite challenges such as foreign exchange and inflation.
  • Segment Performance: The Medical Devices segment experienced double-digit growth in 2024, particularly in Diabetes Care, Structural Heart, Electrophysiology, and Heart Failure, driven by new product launches and increased procedure volumes.
  • Regulatory Developments: Abbott continues to navigate complex regulatory environments, including the European Union's Medical Devices Regulation and In Vitro Diagnostic Regulation, which require significant technical expertise and investment to ensure compliance.

Strategic Initiatives

Abbott's strategic initiatives and capital management activities in 2024 focused on enhancing its product portfolio and maintaining financial strength:

  • Strategic Acquisitions: Abbott focused on strategic acquisitions to enhance its product portfolio, including the acquisition of Bigfoot Biomedical, Inc. to advance diabetes management solutions and Cardiovascular Systems, Inc. to expand its vascular device offerings.
  • Capital Management: Abbott maintained a strong capital management strategy by repurchasing approximately 10.2 million shares for $1.1 billion in 2024 under the 2021 repurchase program. The company declared dividends of $2.24 per share in 2024, reflecting a 7.7% increase from the previous year, and authorized a new $7 billion share repurchase program. Abbott also managed its debt effectively, repaying significant portions of its long-term debt, including the €590 million and $1.05 billion notes upon maturity, and modified its yen-denominated term loan to extend maturity and increase principal.
  • Future Outlook: Abbott plans to continue investing in product development to sustain growth, focusing on expanding its diagnostics, medical devices, and nutritional product lines. The company aims to maintain its market-leading position in key areas and explore new opportunities in emerging markets. Abbott also intends to manage its capital efficiently, ensuring that cash flow from operations exceeds capital expenditures and dividends, while maintaining an investment-grade credit rating.

Challenges and Risks

Abbott faces several challenges and risks that could impact its business operations and financial performance:

  • Regulatory Environment: Abbott operates in a highly regulated environment, with its products subject to comprehensive government regulation by the U.S. FDA and similar international agencies. Compliance with these regulations is costly and affects Abbott's business significantly. Changes in healthcare regulations, such as the introduction of the Medical Devices Regulation in the EU, increase the complexity and cost of compliance.
  • Operational Risks: Abbott faces several business and operational risks, including disruptions to its global supply chain, which could negatively affect its results. The company also depends on sophisticated information systems, making it susceptible to cybersecurity incidents that could have a material adverse effect on its business. Additionally, Abbott's research and development efforts may not succeed, potentially causing revenue and profitability to decline.
  • Economic and Geopolitical Risks: Economic, geopolitical, and industry risks include cost containment efforts that could reduce future revenues and operating income, as well as fluctuations in foreign currency exchange rates that have adversely affected Abbott's financial statements. The expiration or loss of intellectual property protection and licenses may also impact future revenues and operating income. Furthermore, public health crises, such as widespread outbreaks of infectious diseases, pose a risk to Abbott's operations and financial condition.
  • Market Risks: Abbott is exposed to foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, which may adversely affect its financial results. The company enters into hedging arrangements to mitigate some of its foreign currency exposure, but cannot predict changes in exchange rates or its ability to mitigate these risks. Additionally, adverse changes in tax laws, regulations, or interpretations could have a material adverse effect on Abbott's effective tax rate, financial condition, and results of operations.
